description: Figure 10 presents two optical micrographs comparing the microstructures of two Fe-Mn-Al-C lightweight steels tempered at 600°C. Subfigure (a) shows the 9Al-20Mn-1C alloy, where austenite grains exhibit parallel intragranular striations with different crystallographic directions in each grain, alongside dark secondary phases lining the grain boundaries; the caption and associated text report these boundary phases are probably δ + α. Subfigure (b) displays the 9Al-30Mn-1C alloy under identical treatment, similarly revealing parallel striations within austenite grains, but with grain-boundary precipitates identified in the text as κ-carbides. Both images carry a 20 μm scale bar. The figure demonstrates that, despite the presence of these precipitates and striations, neither microstructure achieved significant hardening relative to its hot-rolled condition, in contrast to the fine lamellar morphology with short inter-lamellar spacing observed in 9Al-10Mn-1C at the same temperature, which produced pronounced strengthening. These observations support the conclusion that increasing Mn content from 20 wt% to 30 wt% changes the type of grain-boundary precipitation at 600°C while preserving the characteristic striated austenite morphology, though the nature of these striations requires further investigation.
